{"step_by_step_thinking": "Glycogen synthase is involved in glycogenesis, the synthesis of glycogen from glucose. Glycogen phosphorylase is involved in glycogenolysis, the breakdown of glycogen into glucose. Glucan transferase is not commonly associated with either glycogenesis or glycogenolysis. Phosphoglucomutase is involved in the conversion of glucose-1-phosphate to glucose-6-phosphate, which is a step in both glycogenesis and glycogenolysis. Therefore, the enzyme common to both glycogenesis and glycogenolysis is phosphoglucomutase.", "answer_choice": "D"}
